notes: No list-publishing operator (of five, incl. Carnival) excludes AL. Triumph excludes Bingo (not cards) in AL. State-specific skill test not individually verified; majority-rule predominance likely.
No operator exclusions found. Test not individually verified.

ARS 13-3301(4) sweeps in contests 'of chance or skill'; State v. American Holiday Ass'n (1986) carved out contests decided by entrants' own abilities. Triumph, Toast's own Carnival, AND Papaya exclude AZ from redeemable-cash play (Papaya treats AZ as non-redeemable 'Bonus Cash', and prohibits it outright in its most restrictive terms) — excludedByOperators corrected 2->3 on 2026-07-14 after re-reading the Papaya T&C; Skillz and AviaGames still serve it. AZ regulator active in 2025 C&D wave.

Ark. Code 5-66-113 criminalizes betting on 'any game of hazard or skill'. Excluded by Skillz, AviaGames, Triumph; core consensus red-zone state. Notably NOT excluded by Toast's own Carnival T&Cs despite the wager-on-skill statute — Carnival under-excludes vs peer consensus.

Predominance state, all operators serve it — BUT Penal Code 330 names 'twenty-one' among prohibited banking/percentage card games. Toast's 'no dealer or RNG edge' PvP design is an untested workaround theory. CA banned sweepstakes casinos (AB 831, 2025).
No operator exclusions found.

Gambling = gain contingent 'in whole or in part' on chance (excludes 'legal contests of skill'). Skillz and Triumph exclude CT; AviaGames, Papaya and Carnival serve it — genuine operator split. Live regulated iCasino duopoly; banned sweeps casinos 2025.

Excluded by Skillz, AviaGames and Triumph (Papaya and Carnival serve it). State-run iCasino since 2013. State doctrine source not fetched (Del. code/constitution pages unreachable); classification rests on 3/5 operator consensus.
Triumph explicitly supports DC (excludes only Super Peg Master there); no other operator excludes it.

Fla. law prohibits wagering on contests of skill (participant/spectator line unsettled per Faircloth). Triumph excludes FL; Skillz/Avia/Papaya operate there at scale — high-population contested state.
Named as a predominance state; no operator exclusions.
State v. Prevo 'gambling instinct' test creates ambiguity; all five list-publishing operators nonetheless serve HI (Triumph excludes only Super Peg Master).
No operator exclusions found.
Predominance state, but Triumph's ToS warns devices used for real-cash tournaments in IL 'may be subject to forfeiture'; Triumph excludes Bingo (not cards) in IL.

skillDoctrine: any chance + card games per se chance
notes: IC 35-45-5-1(l): a card game or electronic version 'is a game of chance and may not be considered a bona fide contest of skill.' Skillz, AviaGames and Triumph all exclude card games here while serving non-card games. Cash blackjack is categorically unavailable — no matching-engine argument survives a per-se statute.

Parker-Gordon (Iowa 1931): irrelevant whether game is predominantly chance or skill. Toast's own Carnival T&Cs exclude IA entirely AND Papaya lists Iowa as a fully Prohibited Jurisdiction for Cash Competitions (excludedByOperators corrected 1->2 on 2026-07-14 after re-reading the Papaya T&C — the prior 'Papaya serves IA' note was wrong); Triumph excludes all card games there; Skillz and AviaGames still serve IA without a published card carve-out — split practice.
No operator exclusions found.

'Contested' rests on operator-split basis ALONE: only Triumph excludes KY while Skillz, AviaGames, Papaya and Carnival serve it; no state-specific statutory authority fetched. Kentucky has a history of aggressive litigation against online gaming (not independently verified here).

The ONLY state excluded by all five list-publishing operators, including Toast's own Carnival consumer T&Cs. Dedicated 'gambling by computer' felony exposure for operators (R.S. 14:90.3); LGCB sent 40 C&Ds to unregulated operators in 2025. Hardest no in the country.

17-A MRS 952: contest of chance if chance 'can not be eliminated through the application of skill' — card draws fail this. Skillz, AviaGames AND Papaya all bar card games in Maine; Triumph exits entirely. excludedByOperators kept at 1 (only Triumph is a full-state exclusion; the others are card-game-only). LD 1164 (Jan 2026) makes ME the 8th iCasino state (tribal, launch ~2027).
Ifrah: MD law focuses on whether a wager is placed, disregarding the skill-chance distinction. Yet no operator excludes MD — doctrine and practice diverge; risk is latent rather than enforced.
No operator exclusions found.

Most important enforcement precedent for Toast: MGCB's Oct 2024 C&D named Papaya's 21 Cash (blackjack) as illegal unlicensed internet gaming; Papaya's ToS now exclude MI, Triumph is out, and Toast's own Carnival T&Cs exclude MI too — but Skillz and AviaGames still serve it. Practitioner sources disagree on MI's test (predominance per calgaminglaw; wager-focused per Ifrah). Live iCasino state with the most aggressive regulator.
No operator exclusions found.
'Contested' rests on operator-split basis ALONE: only Triumph excludes MS while Skillz, AviaGames, Papaya and Carnival serve it; no state-specific statutory authority fetched.
Thole v. Westfall: contest can be illegal even if 51%+ skill. All operators serve MO; card games would face a harder material-element argument than puzzle games.

Edelman: MT disallows all commercial online gambling regardless of skill (MCA 23-5-112). First state to ban sweepstakes casinos (SB 555, 2025). Skillz/Avia serve MT and Papaya serves it under its most recent cash terms, though Papaya prohibits MT for Solitaire Cash/Bingo Cash and in its most restrictive card-based version (count kept at 1 = Triumph, since current Papaya real-money terms omit MT) — a striking operator-vs-authority conflict.
Triumph excludes Bingo (not cards) in NE; no other exclusions.

Online poker only; no iCasino. 'Contested' rests on operator-split basis ALONE: only Triumph excludes NV while the other four list-publishing operators serve it; no state-specific statutory authority fetched. Comprehensive gaming-licensing state — unlicensed real-money gaming carries regulatory risk not fully mapped here.
No operator exclusions found.
O'Brien v. Scott predominance. Largest mature iCasino market. Skillz excludes only Dominoes Gold in NJ. Banned sweeps casinos (A5447, 2025) — regulated-market states defend their turf.
Triumph excludes only Super Peg Master in NM.

Penal Law 225.00: outcome depending 'in a material degree upon an element of chance' = contest of chance even if skill dominates. Blackjack's card draw is very hard to argue below 'material degree.' All four operators serve NY (Triumph excludes only Super Peg Master), but the NYAG's 2015 DFS campaign and 2025 sweeps campaign (26 C&Ds, S5935 ban) show enforcement appetite. No legal iCasino.
Predominance state; Triumph excludes Bingo (not cards) in NC.
No operator exclusions found.
Predominance state; Triumph excludes Bingo (not cards) in OH.
No operator exclusions found.
Triumph excludes all card games (and Bingo) in OR while Skillz/Avia serve cards there — card-specific caution signal for blackjack.
Predominance state and the largest iCasino market. Triumph excludes card games in PA — likely deference to the regulated casino regime; Skillz/Avia serve cards there.
Live iCasino state (single-operator model); no skill-game operator exclusions found.

16-19-40 criminalizes betting at 'any game with cards or dice'; SC Supreme Court: gambling turns on the wager, not skill; 2025 appellate ruling against Dragon's Ascent skill game. AviaGames, Triumph AND Toast's own Carnival exclude SC; Skillz still serves it, and Papaya's current Cash terms serve SC although its most restrictive product version (eff. 11 Feb 2025) listed South Carolina as a Prohibited Jurisdiction — count kept at 3 (current Papaya terms serve SC); contested, leaning prohibited for card games.

Excluded by Skillz, AviaGames and Triumph (Papaya and Carnival serve it). SD internet-betting statute (SDCL 22-25A) could not be fetched from official/mirror sources; classification rests on 3/5 operator consensus.

Tenn. Code 39-17-501: profit 'to any degree contingent on chance' = gambling. Triumph excludes TN; Skillz/Avia/Papaya AND Carnival serve it — another state where Toast's own T&Cs under-exclude vs the any-chance statute.

Named as an any-chance/gambling-instinct state by practitioner survey, yet all five list-publishing operators serve TX (Triumph excludes only Super Peg Master). Doctrine and market practice diverge; a card game raises the stakes of that bet.
Surprisingly, no operator excludes UT despite its blanket anti-gambling reputation; no state-specific authority fetched. Treat the 'allowed' as operator-practice, not legal opinion.
No operator exclusions in current website T&Cs.
No operator exclusions found.

RCW 9.46.240 makes internet gambling a class C felony; Ifrah: case law and enforcement 'actually prohibit any online gaming' despite skill-friendly statutory text. Triumph excludes cards, Bingo and pegs in WA; Skillz/Avia/Papaya serve it anyway. The most legally dangerous state that operators still serve.
Live iCasino state; no skill-game operator exclusions found.
No operator exclusions found.
No operator exclusions found.
